<a name="Close"></a>
## func Close

```go
func Close()
```

Close shuts down the logger goroutine. Should be called before program exit if you want to ensure all log messages are flushed.

<a name="Debug"></a>
## func Debug

```go
func Debug(format string, v ...any) bool
```



<a name="Dump"></a>
## func Dump

```go
func Dump(out *os.File, level LevelT, lastBytes int64)
```

Dump writes the current log file to out, filtered by minimum level, and returns without following. When lastBytes \> 0 only the final lastBytes of the file are scanned; the first \(likely partial\) line of that window is discarded so parsing starts on a record boundary. Relative timestamps are anchored to the first entry in the scanned window.

This is the non\-interactive counterpart to Follow: it terminates instead of tailing, which makes it usable from scripts and agents that just want to read the recent log.

<a name="Follow"></a>
## func Follow

```go
func Follow(out *os.File, level LevelT, mode FollowMode)
```

Follow tails the current log file to the given writer, auto\-detecting color support. Filters by minimum level. The mode controls when to stop.

<a name="GetLogFile"></a>
## func GetLogFile

```go
func GetLogFile() string
```

GetLogFile returns the current log file path.

<a name="RotateLogFile"></a>
## func RotateLogFile

```go
func RotateLogFile(path string)
```

RotateLogFile rotates the log file if it exceeds maxLogFileSize. Keeps up to maxLogFiles old files \(e.g. critic.ndjson.log.1, critic.ndjson.log.2\).

<a name="ColorizeFunc"></a>
## type ColorizeFunc

ColorizeFunc wraps a string in the given ANSI color code. Receives the ANSI escape \(e.g. "\\033\[33m"\) and the text to wrap.

```go
type ColorizeFunc func(color, str string) string
```

<a name="FollowMode"></a>
## type FollowMode

FollowMode controls when Follow stops tailing the log.

```go
type FollowMode int
```

<a name="UntilProcessTerminates"></a>

```go
const (
    // UntilProcessTerminates stops following when a FATAL message is seen.
    UntilProcessTerminates FollowMode = iota
    // Forever keeps following regardless of log content.
    Forever
)
```

<a name="LevelT"></a>
## type LevelT

LevelT represents a log level

```go
type LevelT int
```

<a name="DEBUG"></a>

```go
const (
    DEBUG LevelT = iota
    INFO
    WARN
    ERROR
    SUCCESS
    FATAL
)
```


<a name="WithMaxLength"></a>
### func WithMaxLength

```go
func WithMaxLength(maxLength int) *SimpleLogger
```

WithMaxLength returns a logger with a custom max length for string args. Use \-1 for unlimited \(no truncation\).

<a name="WithSessionId"></a>
### func WithSessionId

```go
func WithSessionId(sessionId string) *SimpleLogger
```

WithSessionId returns a logger that includes the session ID in log messages

<a name="WithTopic"></a>
### func WithTopic

```go
func WithTopic(topic string) *SimpleLogger
```

WithTopic returns a SimpleLogger that prepends \[topic\] to all log messages

```go
func (t *SimpleLogger) Error(format string, v ...any) bool
```

Error writes an error log message with topic prefix

<a name="SimpleLogger.Fatal"></a>
### func \(\*SimpleLogger\) Fatal

```go
func (t *SimpleLogger) Fatal(format string, v ...any)
```

Fatal writes a fatal error log message with topic prefix and exits

<a name="SimpleLogger.FatalNoExit"></a>
### func \(\*SimpleLogger\) FatalNoExit

```go
func (t *SimpleLogger) FatalNoExit(format string, v ...any)
```

FatalNoExit writes a FATAL\-level log message without calling os.Exit. Use this when you need Follow\(UntilProcessTerminates\) to detect shutdown but still need to run cleanup code after logging.

<a name="SimpleLogger.Info"></a>
### func \(\*SimpleLogger\) Info

```go
func (t *SimpleLogger) Info(format string, v ...any) bool
```

Info writes an info log message with topic prefix

<a name="SimpleLogger.Success"></a>
### func \(\*SimpleLogger\) Success

```go
func (t *SimpleLogger) Success(format string, v ...any) bool
```

Success writes a success log message at the same filtering level as Warn

<a name="SimpleLogger.Warn"></a>
### func \(\*SimpleLogger\) Warn

```go
func (t *SimpleLogger) Warn(format string, v ...any) bool
```

Warn writes a warning log message with topic prefix

<a name="SimpleLogger.WithCaller"></a>
### func \(\*SimpleLogger\) WithCaller

```go
func (sl *SimpleLogger) WithCaller(file string, line int) *SimpleLogger
```

WithCaller returns a logger that uses the provided caller info. This method allows chaining of WithXXX\(\) calls.

<a name="SimpleLogger.WithLevel"></a>
### func \(\*SimpleLogger\) WithLevel

```go
func (sl *SimpleLogger) WithLevel(level LevelT) *SimpleLogger
```

WithLevel returns a logger that uses the provided log level. This method allows chaining of WithXXX\(\) calls.

<a name="SimpleLogger.WithMaxLength"></a>
### func \(\*SimpleLogger\) WithMaxLength

```go
func (sl *SimpleLogger) WithMaxLength(maxLength int) *SimpleLogger
```

WithMaxLength returns a logger that uses the given max length for string args. Use \-1 for unlimited \(no truncation\), 0 for default.

<a name="SimpleLogger.WithSessionId"></a>
### func \(\*SimpleLogger\) WithSessionId

```go
func (sl *SimpleLogger) WithSessionId(sessionId string) *SimpleLogger
```

WithSessionId returns a SimpleLogger that includes the session ID in all log messages.

<a name="SimpleLogger.WithTopic"></a>
### func \(\*SimpleLogger\) WithTopic

```go
func (sl *SimpleLogger) WithTopic(topic string) *SimpleLogger
```

WithTopic returns a SimpleLogger that prepends \[topic\] to all log messages. This method allows chaining of WithXXX\(\) calls.
